civilization.title: Yarlung
civilization.body: Play as Nyatri Tsenpo, the legendary first king of Tibet who descended from heaven. Designate Sacred Peaks with Great People, perform ancient Chöd ceremonies, and build Sky Burial Grounds to unite heaven and earth.

dawnOfMan.blessing:
Blessings upon you, Nyatri Tsenpo, first among kings—Tsenpo of the Sky-Cord—who descended upon the Yarlung Valley borne on the shoulders of the people, your feet too sacred to touch the earth. From the cradle of Yarlung you raised the standard of kingship and set the order of heaven upon the land. Your palace at Yumbu Lagang rose like a falcon upon the ridge, a beacon for clans to gather and a seed from which Tibetan civilization would unfurl across the high snows.
By your divine wisdom, the mountain gods were given their seats and the winds were yoked to banners of the Wind Horse. You bound valley to valley with oath and custom, decreed rites that returned the noble dead to the open sky, and taught that peaks themselves could be sanctuaries where earth receives the blessing of the heavens. Terraces bit into the slopes, watchtowers crowned the passes, and sacred ways climbed toward the sun and moon—each stone a vow that the sky-cord would never be severed.
Now, divine sovereign, your people call upon you once more. Will you designate new sacred peaks to channel the favor of the heavens? Will you raise palaces and sanctuaries from Yarlung to the world’s edge? Can you build a civilization to stand the test of time?
dawnOfMan.introduction: I am Nyatri Tsenpo, descended from heaven to rule these lands. The sacred peaks bear witness to our meeting.
dawnOfMan.defeat: The heavenly cord is severed... but the mountains remember, and one day another shall descend.

uniques.name: The Cord Uniting Heaven and Earth
uniques.body: When a Great Person is born, spawn a Missionary and designate the nearest Mountain as a Sacred Peak, which grants [ICON_CULTURE] Culture and [ICON_PEACE] Faith. When the Great Person is expended, Sacred Peak is transferred to the nearest Mountain.

uniques.name: Pawo
uniques.civilopedia: Pawos are heroic dancers and mediums in the Tibetan spiritual tradition. Through elaborate costumes and ritualistic performances, they conduct the Chöd ceremony - a practice of offering one's body to demons and spirits to cultivate compassion and overcome ego. These spiritual warriors dance at the boundary between the physical and spiritual worlds, channeling divine energies to benefit all sentient beings.
bullets: Can perform a Chöd Ceremony: one-time ability that expends the Pawo in a city.
For 10 turns, that city gains +33%[ICON_GREAT_PEOPLE]Great Person Rate per Great Work it contains and +50% theming bonus.
If the city is within 2 tiles of a Mountain, gain +50%[ICON_TOURISM]Tourism from Great Works for the duration.

uniques.name: Sky Burial Ground
uniques.body: Yields +2[ICON_CULTURE]Culture and +2[ICON_PEACE]Faith, plus +1 of each for every adjacent Mountain and Great Person Improvement. Yields +1[ICON_TOURISM]Tourism after researching Archaeology.
uniques.civilopedia: Sky burial grounds are sacred sites where the deceased are offered to vultures and the elements, returning the body to nature and sending the spirit skyward. These charnel grounds serve as powerful reminders of impermanence and the cycle of life and death. Located near mountains - the abodes of gods and spirits - they represent the intersection of earth and heaven, making them places of profound spiritual significance in Tibetan culture.
bullets: Yields +2[ICON_CULTURE]Culture and +2[ICON_PEACE]Faith, plus +1 of each for every adjacent Mountain and Great Person Improvement.

credits.name: Coiot
credits.role: Art, Author, Coding, Design, Research, Writing
